reference_note: Gold-standard outputs produced by applying the shared-prompts/presets/hyper.txt rules to each pp_inputs.json entry. Plays the same role for /post-process scoring that hand-corrected Scribe v2 plays for /transcribe scoring.

references.069538f2f06a: Hey, can you remember into the future that I like to use dark themes?
references.420c6e7a09cd: Searching online with the Exa MCP server, can you basically figure out a way to add local models to the Windows application? Kind of like the Gemma models. Would they be running on a GPU or something? How could they be easily integrated? Basically, search pretty extensively and heavily until you find a pretty good solution or approach here. references.521a29cf3ccb: Now, a skill that I find really handy.
references.7fa0c1f6633f: I definitely want to make a video about compact engineering, compound engineering.
references.9806c316d1d0:
For the homepage, can you remove the thing that says two kinds of work? I noticed something weird about Codex's /goal command. For the second slide where it says example Notion integration. Actually, leave the part about every story is known upfront.

For the unfolding, this seems pretty good for 'a goal must be verifiable'. This also seems pretty good. Compaction makes the thread richer. As for 'compaction is what makes #8 possible', remove that part because I don't think it's required.

Also, for the fonts, we have this weird italic font. Can we have a normal font instead? I mean, still the handwritten font, but go for a different style instead. Maybe you can make a page that shows me five to ten different styles, and I pick the one that I like.

Can we remove the part about 'ralph leads to this for breakfast'? For 'the goal carries a hypothesis', I feel like this could be added somewhere else. 'Peak without breaking the flow' is also pretty good. The hybrid also seems good as well.

And I think I want to add another slide and another point and basically say, okay, so when it comes to unfolding, unfolding can be good, but you can get a suboptimal solution, in which case this leads to the idea of a scrappy copy first, where you basically make this copy, and you should be willing to throw it away and then re-implement it in a way that works better with the rest of the code base. I want to explore this idea in a bit more detail. Can you help explore it with me somehow?
references.a1a5c3b61e2f:
Can you make a deck with a whole bunch of slides all about this? Have slides with different representations, so each slide has three variations, and I can pick the ones that I like, and then you will make a final version of the slides with all these concepts kind of attached in an order, and then we can use this instead. And then I can just have talking points instead.

I think this seems like a much better approach because it seems like a better approach moving forwards. Do the things that we discussed about here, basically.
references.a883a4831dfd: Okay, we have a workflow-like skill. Can we describe some of the capabilities to this skill? So it's slightly better at actually making these, in a way.
references.b4bd4fe156c2:
Okay, so we previously talked about designing components with Claude Code. There will be a video linked down below for that.

And essentially what we did is we created an HTML artifact with the different design variations that we wanted, and then just picked the one we liked, and then gave it back to Claude Code, and it integrated it into the rest of the application.

Now, we can take this one step further and make our artifacts interactive, and this can be especially handy for making more difficult decisions.

So there are a couple ways that we can make it interactive, but let's go through the first way. Now, let's use the same example of designing components. So I have this page of the application over here, and it could look better. I don't really know what better looks like, so I will tell Claude Code to make 10 variations of this page.
references.d0036702401b: So I also added the following domain to Bento. Can you send the feature emails from there instead? And also for the newsletter confirm thing, we should be using the new domain and sending them back to that. And also, can we have the homepage automatically redirect to this page on which the actual thing is, in a way? So they would go back to the wait list, and they would basically be subscribing from there essentially. And then can you also come up with a design for the email so it kind of follows this new design that we're going after for agentengineer.pro?
references.synth_code_dictation: Okay, so let's create a function called process_data that takes a list parameter and returns a sorted version. Make sure to handle the empty case by returning an empty list. Use the sorted built-in with a key argument lambda x: x.timestamp.
